core_identity.title: Solving Quadratic Equations Using the Quadratic Formula
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: quadratic formula and discriminant (nature of roots)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use the quadratic formula to solve any quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0, calculate the discriminant D = b^2 - 4ac, and interpret D to decide whether there are two real roots, one real root, or no real roots. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can one universal formula solve any quadratic — and how can the discriminant predict the type of roots before we even finish the calculation?

knowing_world.focus: Use the quadratic formula as a universal method for solving quadratics in the form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, and use the discriminant to predict the nature of the solutions. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
key_concepts: Quadratic equation in standard form: ax^2 + bx + c = 0
If D < 0: no real roots (complex roots)
activities: Solve x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0 using the quadratic formula.
Try a quadratic with no real roots such as x^2 + x + 1 = 0 and describe what the discriminant tells you.

human_story.focus: The quadratic formula dates back to ancient mathematical thinking (including Babylonian and Greek work) where people needed systematic methods to solve area and length problems. A universal method mattered because it worked even when a quadratic wouldn’t factorise neatly. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
justice_prompts: Why might a universal method for all quadratics have been important before calculators and modern algebra tools?

expressive_self.focus: Communicate the meaning of each part of the formula and the role of the discriminant through story, visuals, or performance. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}
activities: Write a short story or comic strip titled: “A Day in the Life of the Discriminant.”
Design a visual poster showing how each part of the formula contributes to the solution.
Create a rap or spoken word poem explaining how the quadratic formula works.

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive quadratic formula calculator to input values for a, b and c and see the solution steps and roots, then investigate how changing b^2 - 4ac changes the nature of the roots. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
tools: Canva embed: Interactive quadratic formula calculator (step-by-step)
activities: Try different values for a, b and c and observe how the roots change.
Watch what happens as the discriminant b^2 - 4ac changes sign.
Try to make the discriminant exactly zero and explain what that means for the roots.
design_challenges: Design three quadratics: one with D > 0, one with D = 0, and one with D < 0. Show your discriminant calculations and predict the root type before solving.

regulation_supports: Form a “human equation”: in groups of three, represent a, b, and c, then act out each step of the quadratic formula using cards or strings to visualise operations.

pathways.name: Final Task - Three Quadratics with the Formula
pathways.focus: Choose three different quadratic equations and solve them using the quadratic formula, explaining steps clearly and using the discriminant to interpret solution types. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}
products: Three different quadratic equations selected by the learner
Full solutions using the quadratic formula with correct substitution and careful bracketing
Discriminant calculation for each equation and interpretation of what it predicts
One created real-world problem that results in a quadratic equation (optional extension)
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding — Explains each step of the quadratic formula and the role of the discriminant
Fluency & Accuracy — Uses correct operations, brackets carefully, and simplifies accurately
Application — Creates or connects to a context that leads to a quadratic equation
Reflection — Explains when the quadratic formula is more useful than factorisation

required: Working confidently with squares and square roots
Solving linear equations (for checking and interpreting roots)

builds_toward: Completing the square and the derivation of the quadratic formula
Interpreting discriminant and linking to quadratic graphs (number of x-intercepts)
Solving quadratics with irrational roots and exact form answers
Complex numbers as solutions when D < 0 (advanced extension)
Quadratic modelling in geometry and real-life contexts
